building_row.notes: Archer is one of the stronger amenity packages in the set and the official fee disclosure is better than average, but the building is still a weak fit for a quiet-first move. packet_markdown:
# Archer Hollywood building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: archer_hollywood
- Address: 1718 N Las Palmas Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://archerhwood.com/
- Management company: Nexen Properties
- Recommended status: research_complete
- Last updated: 2026-04-05

## Current Stage 2 Unit Context
- Unit(s) reviewed: 231 (0 bd, 626 sqft), 331 (0 bd, 626 sqft), 431 (0 bd, 626 sqft), 235 (0 bd, 750 sqft), 112 (0 bd, 562 sqft), 505 (0 bd, 601 sqft), 219 (1 bd, 628 sqft), 319 (1 bd, 628 sqft), 218 (1 bd, 667 sqft), 318 (1 bd, 667 sqft), 107 (1 bd, 645 sqft), 215 (1 bd, 674 sqft), 133 (1 bd, 766 sqft), 239 (1 bd, 766 sqft), 514 (1 bd, 668 sqft), 111 (1 bd, 667 sqft), 323 (1 bd, 855 sqft)
- Current pricing observed: Current Stage 2 rows show 17 qualifying units. Studios run about $2,095 to $2,715 base rent, and current 1 bedroom rows run about $2,320 to $2,995 base rent, including a 1 bed plus den at the top of the visible range. Apartments.com currently shows overall studio to 2 bedroom pricing from about $1,960 to $3,950 and notes short-term lease availability, while the official site advertises up to $2,500 in free rent but does not expose a clean self-serve unit table.
- Immediate unit-specific open questions: Need to verify whether the most relevant available 1 bedroom stacks face Hollywood Blvd, the roof decks, the pool deck, or quieter interior exposures; whether the 2025 hot-water, heater, and maintenance complaints reflect an unresolved current issue or a past management-change period; whether the advertised specials and lease terms are quoted cleanly on the exact unit without renewal or term surprises; whether there is a real after-hours human presence beyond daytime leasing staff; and whether rooftop, screening-room, or street-nightlife noise carries into the residential stack at night.

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 2 (high, evidence count 4)
- Management: 2 (medium, evidence count 4)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 3)
- Community stability: 3 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: medium
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] Current official materials confirm a very strong amenity package with coworking, conference space, a screening room, two roof decks, pool and spa amenities, parcel lockers, and in-unit washers and dryers. Source: Archer Hollywood amenities page (https://archerhwood.com/amenities/). Note: The current amenities page lists a dedicated coworking space with conference room, private screening room with Karaoke, two roof decks, pool deck and spa with cabanas, double-level fitness center, parcel lockers, and in-unit stackable washer and dryer.
- [official] The official general-information page is more transparent than average on baseline fees and pet terms, but the home page still warns that non-lease amenities, fees, or concessions can change. Source: Archer Hollywood general information and home pages (https://archerhwood.com/general-information/). Note: The general-information page lists deposit as 1 month rent, application fee as $58 per adult, max two pets, $50 monthly pet rent, and $500 refundable pet deposit per pet, while the home page says amenities, fees, or concessions not included in the lease agreement are subject to change without notice.
- [official] Official location marketing explicitly centers Hollywood Boulevard nightlife and immediate bar and restaurant adjacency, which is a real everyday noise risk for this search. Source: Archer Hollywood neighborhood page (https://archerhwood.com/neighborhood/). Note: The neighborhood page says the property sits at the intersection of Hollywood Blvd and N. Las Palmas and that nightlife, bars, and restaurants are steps away.
- [official] Current listing feeds corroborate a busy living environment with short-term lease options rather than a purely conventional long-term residential profile. Source: Apartments.com listing (https://www.apartments.com/archer-hollywood-los-angeles-ca/zrmncdx/). Note: Apartments.com currently shows 13 to 15 month leases, short-term lease availability, property manager on site, maintenance on site, and a Busy sound profile with traffic and businesses marked busy.
- [public_record] Public-record and local-logic signals point to a noisy, active area and confirm the building is a 2021 six-story mixed-use project over three basement levels. Source: Redfin public record and Local Logic profile (https://www.redfin.com/CA/Los-Angeles/1718-N-Las-Palmas-Ave-90028/home/167572236). Note: Redfin shows a Jan. 2021 permit for a new six-story mixed-use building over three basement levels and current Local Logic scores of 2.8 out of 10 for noisy area and 6.6 out of 10 for quite busy, along with later EV charger permits.
- [review] At least some resident feedback is strongly positive on security presence, parking, cleanliness, and maintenance responsiveness, which prevents an automatic worst-case reading. Source: Chamber of Commerce review rollup (https://www.chamberofcommerce.com/united-states/california/los-angeles/apartment-building/2023045264-archer-hollywood). Note: The Chamber page shows a 3.7 rating across 43 reviews and includes a resident review praising security guards, cleaning staff, rooftop areas, pool, theater, work rooms, underground parking, and management communication.
- [review] Recent review snippets describe maintenance frustration, hot-water and heater problems, and a lease-term surprise, which materially lowers management confidence even after accounting for the positive reviews. Source: MapQuest review rollup from Yelp (https://www.mapquest.com/us/california/archer-hollywood-521717383). Note: Recent snippets include a Dec. 2025 complaint about new management and long-running lack of warm water, a Mar. 2025 complaint about a 12 month lease changing to 13 months, a Mar. 2025 complaint about no working heater, and a June 2024 complaint about leasing follow-up and call-center confusion.

## Open questions
- Need to verify whether the most relevant available 1 bedroom stacks face Hollywood Blvd, the roof decks, the pool deck, or quieter interior exposures.
- Need to verify whether the 2025 hot-water, heater, and maintenance complaints reflect an unresolved current issue or a past management-change period.
- Need to verify whether the advertised specials and lease terms are quoted cleanly on the exact unit without renewal or term surprises.
- Need to verify whether there is a real after-hours human presence beyond daytime leasing staff.
- Need to verify whether rooftop, screening-room, or street-nightlife noise carries into the residential stack at night.

## Notes
Archer is one of the stronger amenity packages in the set and the official fee disclosure is better than average, but the building is still a weak fit for a quiet-first move. Official materials openly market immediate Hollywood nightlife adjacency, Apartments.com currently labels the sound profile busy and offers short-term lease options, and Redfin Local Logic flags the area as noisy and quite busy. Management confidence also stays constrained because recent review snippets describe hot-water loss, heater failure, maintenance frustration, and at least one lease-term surprise, even though other reviews praise security, staff helpfulness, and amenity quality. That mix keeps the building researched but not strong enough to prioritize for a tour right now.
